description: A call with no read or overall timeout hangs forever when the peer accepts the connection then stalls.
body: Failure mode: a client sets no read/overall timeout (or relies on an infinite default), so when a server accepts the connection but never responds (overload, deadlock, black hole) the caller blocks indefinitely, parking a thread and a connection until the whole pool is consumed and the service stalls. Detection signals: threads stuck in socket read; latency climbing to infinity for one dependency; pool acquisition blocking; no timeout in the client config. Do not depend on library default timeouts. Safer fallback: set connect, read/write, and overall deadlines from the operation SLO, and verify with a black-hole test server that the call fails by the deadline instead of hanging.
